description: Complete the core request and navigation flow, using a robust state manager like Riverpod/Provider to track a mock ambulance trip.

userStories.title: Implement Core Trip State Management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a robust state management solution to track the current phase of an ambulance request so the UI can update appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a state manager (e.g., TripProvider or TripBloc) to hold trip state
Define states: idle, request_incoming, accepted, navigating_to_patient, patient_picked_up, navigating_to_hospital, completed
Add methods to transition between these states
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Service to Handle Mock Backend Trip Updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer to simulate a backend that dispatches trips and updates their status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a TripService that can trigger mock incoming requests
Connect TripService to the state manager created in US-001 so it can dispatch updates
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate State with Incoming Request Screen
userStories.description: As a driver, I want the incoming request screen to be tied to real application state so that when I slide to accept, the app knows the trip has started.
acceptanceCriteria: Update IncomingRequestScreen to listen to the new trip state
Connect the SlideToAcceptButton to trigger the state transition to 'accepted'
Automatically navigate to ActiveNavigationScreen upon successful acceptance
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Active Navigation Screen UI
userStories.description: As a driver, I need a screen that shows the map and trip details while I am navigating to the patient or hospital.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ActiveNavigationScreen widget
Include MapBackgroundMock as the base layer
Add a floating card showing ETA, distance, and patient details
Include primary action buttons (e.g., 'Arrived at Patient') that change based on state
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate Active Navigation Screen with State
userStories.description: As a driver, I want the Active Navigation screen to update its buttons and information based on what part of the trip I am currently on.
acceptanceCriteria: Connect ActiveNavigationScreen UI actions to the trip state manager
Ensure action buttons trigger the correct state transitions (e.g., 'Arrived' -> 'patient_picked_up')
Ensure UI gracefully returns to Dashboard when trip state is 'completed'
Typecheck passes
